steps: Log in to your Airtable account (or create a free account at https://airtable.com/signup)
Give your token a descriptive name
Under 'Scopes', select the permissions your token needs (e.g. data.records:read, data.records:write, schema.bases:read)
Under 'Access', choose which bases or workspaces the token can access
Copy the token immediately — it is shown only once

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: Personal Access Token
credential_fields.description: Your Airtable personal access token. Used as a Bearer token in the Authorization header.

notes: Free plan available with generous limits for personal use
Personal Access Tokens replace the deprecated legacy API key (migrated as of Feb 2024)
The token is shown only once at creation — save it securely
Tokens start with 'pat' followed by your workspace ID and a random secret
Scope-limited tokens are recommended: grant only the permissions your app needs
Rate limit: 5 requests per second per base on the free plan

test_endpoint.description: List all bases accessible to the token
